title: Create detections and perform investigations using Microsoft Sentinel
meta: 8 modules · 56 units · Analytics, automation, playbooks, incidents, UEBA, ASIM, workbooks, and content management

focusAreas.title: Detection and response
focusAreas.description: Build analytics rules, automate triage, run playbooks, and manage security incidents.

focusAreas.title: Analytics and content
focusAreas.description: Apply UEBA, ASIM, KQL, workbooks, Content Hub solutions, and repository-based deployment.

modules.title: Threat detection with Microsoft Sentinel analytics
modules.focus: Focus: rule types, templates, custom logic, incident creation, entities, and lifecycle management.

units.title: Introduction
units.bodyHtml:
Sentinel analytics turns ingested telemetry into alerts and incidents. Choose a detection method, define actionable logic, map entities, tune noise, and maintain the rule as threats and sources evolve.

Official moduleObjectives and prerequisites

units.title: Exercise - Detect threats with Microsoft Sentinel analytics
units.bodyHtml:
Deploy a Sentinel workspace and test VM, install the Azure Activity solution, and connect subscription activity through Azure Policy. Retain the resources for the later detection exercise and monitor Azure cost.

Official exercisePrepare the lab

units.title: What is Microsoft Sentinel Analytics?
units.bodyHtml:
Analytics correlates signals from endpoints, identities, networks, cloud services, and threat intelligence. Use cases include compromised accounts, suspicious behavior, exfiltration, insider threats, incident investigation, and hunting. Filter templates by severity, type, ATT&CK tactics, and source.

Official lessonAnalytics purpose and page

units.title: Types of analytics rules
units.bodyHtml:
TypeUse
Scheduled / NRTCustom KQL on a schedule or near real time.
FusionML correlation of low-fidelity signals into multistage incidents.
Microsoft securityIncidents from connected Microsoft security alerts.
ML / anomalyMicrosoft-managed behavioral detections.
Threat intelligenceObservable matches against organizational events.
Exam takeaway: scheduled rules are customizable; managed Fusion and ML logic is not.
Official lessonAnalytics rule families

units.title: Create an analytics rule from templates
units.bodyHtml:
Templates expose severity, required sources, ATT&CK mapping, and rule type. Scheduled and Microsoft security templates allow query or alert-filter tuning; Fusion and ML templates are generally enabled or disabled. A disabled Create rule action often indicates a missing connector.

Official lessonTemplate workflow

units.title: Create an analytics rule from wizard
units.bodyHtml:
1. Define metadata, severity, and ATT&CK mapping.
  2. Enter KQL and set frequency, lookback, threshold, and grouping.
  3. Map query columns to entities and custom details.
  4. Configure incidents and automated response.
  5. Validate and monitor the rule.

Entity mapping powers correlation, graphs, timelines, and investigation pivots.

Official lessonCustom rule wizard

units.title: Manage analytics rules
units.bodyHtml:
Enable, disable, edit, duplicate, export, delete, and monitor rules. Tune KQL, thresholds, grouping, suppression, and exclusions using false-positive and missed-detection evidence. Document ownership, data dependencies, expected volume, and change history.

Official lessonRule lifecycle and tuning

units.title: Exercise - Detect threats with Microsoft Sentinel analytics
units.bodyHtml:
Create a detection for successful Azure VM deletion, map caller and IP entities, generate test activity, and verify the alert and incident.

Official exerciseBuild and validate a detection

units.title: Summary
units.bodyHtml:
Effective analytics begins with the right data, uses the appropriate rule type, maps entities, creates actionable incidents, and is continuously measured and tuned.

Official moduleSummary and assessment

modules.title: Automation in Microsoft Sentinel
modules.focus: Focus: automation triggers, conditions, ordered actions, expiration, and incident orchestration.

units.title: Introduction
units.bodyHtml:
Automation reduces repetitive triage and coordinates consistent incident handling. Automation rules manage incident properties and invoke playbooks for broader response.

Official moduleAutomation objectives

units.title: Understand automation options
units.bodyHtml:
Rules trigger on incident creation or update, or alert creation. Conditions can inspect provider, rule, severity, status, tags, and owner. Native actions assign owners, change status or severity, add tags, and run playbooks. Use playbooks for APIs, enrichment, notification, ticketing, or containment.

Official lessonRules versus playbooks

units.title: Create automation rules
units.bodyHtml:
1. Select a trigger and scoped conditions.
  2. Add actions in the intended order.
  3. Set order relative to other rules.
  4. Optionally define expiration.
  5. Enable, test, and monitor.

Order matters because an earlier rule can change properties evaluated by a later rule.

Official lessonCreate and prioritize rules

units.title: Module assessment
units.bodyHtml:
Review triggers, conditions, native actions, playbook execution, ordering, expiration, and the boundary between Sentinel automation and Logic Apps.

Official assessmentAutomation knowledge check

units.title: Summary and resources
units.bodyHtml:
Use explicit automation rules for deterministic changes and governed playbooks when a workflow crosses services or requires complex logic.

Official moduleSummary and resources

modules.title: Threat response with Microsoft Sentinel playbooks
modules.focus: Focus: Logic Apps SOAR, Sentinel triggers, permissions, and real-time or on-demand execution.

units.title: Introduction
units.bodyHtml:
Playbooks are Logic Apps workflows for enrichment, notification, ticketing, containment, and remediation beyond native incident-property changes.

Official moduleSOAR objectives

units.title: Exercise - Create a Microsoft Sentinel playbook
units.bodyHtml:
Deploy Sentinel and test resources, connect Azure Activity, and create an NRT rule for successful VM deletion to supply incidents for the playbook exercise.

Official exercisePrepare analytics and test data

units.title: What are Microsoft Sentinel playbooks?
units.bodyHtml:
A playbook has a Logic Apps trigger followed by conditions and actions. Sentinel triggers operate on incidents, alerts, or entities; connectors call Microsoft and third-party services. Prefer managed identities and least-privilege RBAC over personal credentials.

Official lessonPlaybook architecture

units.title: Trigger a playbook in real-time
units.bodyHtml:
Use an automation rule to run an incident- or alert-triggered playbook. Grant Sentinel permission to run the Logic App and grant its identity only required permissions. Design for idempotency, retries, rate limits, partial failure, logging, and visible incident comments.

Official lessonAutomated execution

units.title: Run playbooks on demand
units.bodyHtml:
Run eligible playbooks manually from incidents, alerts, or entities when analyst judgment should precede response. This suits enrichment and high-impact containment that should not be automatic.

Official lessonManual response

units.title: Exercise - Create a Microsoft Sentinel playbook
units.bodyHtml:
Create an incident-triggered Logic App, add response actions, authorize connections, attach it through automation, generate a test incident, and inspect run history.

Official exerciseBuild and test a playbook

units.title: Summary
units.bodyHtml:
Safe SOAR requires least privilege, explicit triggers, controlled automation rules, resilient workflows, and visible execution results.

Official moduleSummary and assessment

modules.title: Security incident management in Microsoft Sentinel
modules.focus: Focus: incident structure, evidence, entities, ownership, status, severity, tasks, and resolution.

units.title: Introduction
units.bodyHtml:
An incident groups related alerts, evidence, and entities into a case. Analysts triage priority, establish scope, document findings, respond, and close with an accurate classification.

Official moduleIncident objectives

units.title: Exercise - Set up the Azure environment
units.bodyHtml:
Deploy the lab, connect Azure Activity, retain the environment, and validate actual log arrival before relying on the investigation workflow.

Official exercisePrepare the investigation lab

units.title: Understand incidents
units.bodyHtml:
Analytics alerts become incidents when creation is enabled. Related alerts can be grouped by entities, tactics, timing, or rule settings. Severity, status, owner, tags, source, and timestamps support triage.

Exam takeaway: an alert is a detection signal; an incident is the analyst case containing one or more alerts.
Official lessonAlerts and incidents

units.title: Incident evidence and entities
units.bodyHtml:
Evidence includes events, queries, timelines, comments, tasks, bookmarks, and entities. Entity mapping creates pivots such as account, host, IP, URL, file, process, and resource. Use the investigation graph and entity pages to follow relationships and establish an attack path.

Official lessonEvidence and graph pivots

units.title: Incident management
units.bodyHtml:
1. Assign ownership and validate severity.
  2. Set New, Active, or Closed status.
  3. Use tags, comments, and tasks for handoff.
  4. Investigate alerts, entities, timelines, and similar incidents.
  5. Run response actions and close with reason and classification.

Accurate closure classification improves metrics and tuning.

Official lessonTriage through closure

units.title: Exercise - Investigate an incident
units.bodyHtml:
Generate suspicious activity, locate the incident, assign and activate it, examine alerts and entities, pivot through logs, record findings, and resolve it with the correct classification.

Official exerciseEnd-to-end investigation

units.title: Summary
units.bodyHtml:
A disciplined workflow preserves evidence, makes decisions auditable, accelerates response, and feeds quality improvements back into detections.

Official moduleSummary and assessment

modules.title: Identify threats with Behavioral Analytics
modules.focus: Focus: UEBA baselines, entities, timelines, behavioral insights, investigation priority, and anomalies.

units.title: Introduction
units.bodyHtml:
User and Entity Behavior Analytics adds behavioral context to users, hosts, IPs, applications, and other entities so analysts can prioritize activity that static rules may miss.

Official moduleUEBA objectives

units.title: Understand behavioral analytics
units.bodyHtml:
Sentinel builds baselines from entity history, peer groups, organization behavior, time, location, devices, and environments. ML adds anomalous context, sensitivity, relationships, and potential blast radius. The 0-10 investigation priority score is a prioritization signal, not proof of compromise.

Official lessonBaselines and scoring

units.title: Explore entities
units.bodyHtml:
Sentinel merges identifiers such as GUID, UPN, account name, and domain when enough context exists. Entity pages combine identifying facts, a timeline of alerts, bookmarks and activities, and behavioral insights from sign-in, audit, Office, Syslog, SecurityEvent, and BehaviorAnalytics data.

Official lessonEntity identity and timelines

units.title: Display entity behavior information
units.bodyHtml:
Search entities and review risk context, activity trends, peers, related alerts, and anomalies. Pivot from incidents or hunting results into the same entity view to keep investigation context connected.

Official lessonEntity behavior views

units.title: Use Anomaly detection analytical rule templates
units.bodyHtml:
Anomaly templates use Microsoft-managed ML. Review required sources, anomaly details, contributing events, and entity context; combine anomaly evidence with other signals instead of treating it as a verdict.

Official lessonManaged anomaly detections

units.title: Module assessment
units.bodyHtml:
Review baselines, peer comparison, priority scoring, entity identifiers and merging, entity pages, timelines, insights, and anomaly interpretation.

Official assessmentBehavioral analytics knowledge check

units.title: Summary and resources
units.bodyHtml:
UEBA depends on complete telemetry, correct entity mapping, and analyst validation of anomalous context.

Official moduleSummary and resources

modules.title: Data normalization in Microsoft Sentinel
modules.focus: Focus: ASIM schemas and parsers, parameterized KQL, custom parsers, and DCR transformations.

units.title: Introduction
units.bodyHtml:
Normalization maps vendor-specific schemas into a common semantic model so one detection, hunt, or workbook can operate across sources.

Official moduleASIM objectives

units.title: Understand data normalization
units.bodyHtml:
ASIM defines schemas, fields, values, aliases, and parsers for domains such as authentication, network, DNS, process, file, and web activity. Source-specific parsers translate native tables; unifying parsers combine enabled sources.

Official lessonASIM architecture

units.title: Use ASIM Parsers
units.bodyHtml:
Call an ASIM unifying parser like a KQL function and apply supported parameters early. Filtering within the parser is usually more efficient than filtering after all sources are unioned.

_Im_Authentication(starttime=ago(1d)) | where EventResult == "Failure" | summarize Attempts=count() by TargetUsername, SrcIpAddr
Official lessonQuery normalized data

units.title: Understand parameterized KQL functions
units.bodyHtml:
Stored functions accept typed parameters and return tabular data. Use parameters for time, source, user, IP, or severity filters. Centralized logic improves reuse, but changes affect every dependent rule, workbook, and query.

Official lessonReusable KQL functions

units.title: Create an ASIM Parser
units.bodyHtml:
1. Select the schema and map native fields.
  2. Normalize values and types.
  3. Add filter parameters and metadata.
  4. Test representative, missing, and malformed records.
  5. Include the parser in the appropriate unifying parser.

Quality means semantic correctness, coverage, performance, and ASIM content compatibility.

Official lessonBuild a custom parser

units.title: Configure Azure Monitor Data Collection Rules
units.bodyHtml:
DCR transformations use KQL at ingestion to filter, reshape, enrich, or route records. The query begins with source and must return columns compatible with the destination. Filtering can reduce cost, but discarded data cannot be recovered.

Official lessonIngestion transformations

units.title: Module assessment
units.bodyHtml:
Review schemas, source and unifying parsers, normalized fields, parameterized functions, parser creation, DCR transformations, and permanent ingestion filtering.

Official assessmentNormalization knowledge check

units.title: Summary and resources
units.bodyHtml:
ASIM makes content portable; parameterized parsers improve performance, and DCR transformations control data before workspace storage.

Official moduleSummary and resources

modules.title: Query, visualize, and monitor data in Microsoft Sentinel
modules.focus: Focus: Logs, KQL, workbook templates, custom visualizations, and parameters.

units.title: Introduction
units.bodyHtml:
Logs supports ad hoc analysis; workbooks turn KQL into reusable interactive views for monitoring, investigation, and reporting.

Official moduleQuery and visualization objectives

units.title: Exercise - Query and visualize data with Microsoft Sentinel Workbooks
units.bodyHtml:
Deploy the Sentinel lab and Azure Activity connector. Confirm records in AzureActivity before building workbook elements.

Official exercisePrepare workbook data

units.title: Monitor and visualize data
units.bodyHtml:
Workbooks combine text, parameters, KQL, metrics, links, tabs, and visualizations. Use charts for comparisons and trends, grids for exact records, and retain drill-down to underlying evidence.

Official lessonWorkbook capabilities

units.title: Query data using Kusto Query Language
units.bodyHtml:
Start with the correct table and time range, filter early, project needed columns, derive fields, aggregate, and order results. Validate cost and meaning before embedding queries in content.

AzureActivity | where TimeGenerated > ago(24h) | summarize Operations=count() by Caller, ActivityStatusValue
Official lessonKQL in Logs

units.title: Use default Microsoft Sentinel Workbooks
units.bodyHtml:
Content Hub solutions install workbook templates. Save a template, confirm required tables have data, and review parameters and queries before trusting its visuals. Saved copies are editable Azure resources.

Official lessonTemplate workbooks

units.title: Create a new Microsoft Sentinel Workbook
units.bodyHtml:
1. Define audience and question.
  2. Add time or selection parameters.
  3. Add KQL and suitable visuals.
  4. Reference parameters safely.
  5. Save with meaningful resource placement and access control.
Official lessonCustom workbooks

units.title: Exercise - Visualize data using Microsoft Sentinel Workbooks
units.bodyHtml:
Explore Azure Activity logs, save its workbook template, use filters and tables, enter edit mode, and add a KQL-backed parameter to drive interactive analysis.

Official exerciseInteractive workbook view

units.title: Summary
units.bodyHtml:
Reliable workbooks use validated KQL, relevant parameters, appropriate visual encoding, evidence drill-down, and Azure resource governance.

Official moduleSummary and assessment

modules.title: Manage content in Microsoft Sentinel
modules.focus: Focus: Content Hub solutions and repository-based content deployment.

units.title: Introduction
units.bodyHtml:
Sentinel content includes connectors, analytics, hunting queries, workbooks, parsers, watchlists, automation, and playbooks. Content Hub packages solutions; repositories support version-controlled deployment.

Official lessonContent objectives

units.title: Use solutions from the content hub
units.bodyHtml:
Search by product, provider, support model, type, or status. Install a solution, review content and dependencies, configure connectors, and enable only needed rules. Review release notes and customizations before update or removal.

Official lessonSolution lifecycle

units.title: Use repositories for deployment
units.bodyHtml:
Connect GitHub or Azure DevOps, select content paths, and use the generated workflow or pipeline to validate and deploy supported content. Protect branches, require review, separate development and production, keep secrets out of source, and monitor failures.

Official lessonSentinel content as code

units.title: Module assessment
units.bodyHtml:
Review Content Hub discovery and lifecycle, solution dependencies, content types, repository connections, automated deployment, and source-control governance.

Official assessmentContent knowledge check

units.title: Summary and resources
units.bodyHtml:
Use Content Hub for supported packages and repositories for controlled, repeatable deployment of customized content across environments.

Official moduleSummary and resources
